sections.model_description: DiagAssist-Radiology-v2 is a deep learning model that analyzes chest X-ray images to detect pneumonia, tuberculosis, and lung nodules. The model outputs a probability score and a heatmap overlay showing regions of interest. In high-confidence cases (score > 0.95), the system flags the case as positive automatically in the hospital PACS system.
sections.intended_use: Designed as a diagnostic aid for radiologists in hospital settings to prioritize and triage chest X-ray readings.
sections.out_of_scope_use: Not intended for pediatric radiology or CT/MRI modalities.
sections.limitations: Performance may be reduced for portable/bedside X-rays with lower image quality. The model has not been validated on X-rays from patients with implanted devices.
sections.bias_analysis: The model was tested across patient age groups (18-40, 41-60, 60+) with AUC differences of less than 0.02. Gender-specific performance was not separately evaluated. Racial and ethnic subgroup analysis was not performed.
sections.training_data: Trained on 200,000 chest X-rays from three academic medical centers in the United States. Labels were derived from radiology reports using an NLP extraction pipeline. The NLP pipeline accuracy was not reported.
sections.evaluation_metrics: Sensitivity: 0.96, Specificity: 0.94, AUC: 0.98 for pneumonia detection. Performance on TB and nodule detection was not separately reported.
sections.ethical_considerations: This is a medical AI system with direct patient safety implications. Misdiagnosis could lead to delayed treatment. The model should be used as a second opinion tool only.
sections.environmental_impact: Training on 8x A100 GPUs for 72 hours. Estimated 120 kg CO2eq.
sections.citation: Smith et al., 2024 — Deep Learning for Automated Chest X-ray Interpretation.
sections.license: Research License — Clinical deployment requires regulatory approval
